bullets.text: Q2 2015 combined ratio was 92.5; Property business (first full quarter in monthly reporting) posted a CR of ~92 excluding ~7.5 points of intangible amortization, with an improving monthly trend through June reaching ~85.

Net premium growth of 13% including Property for the first time; Direct auto PIF grew ~7% and Commercial Lines grew mid-teens; Agency auto showed early positive directional change in new business but still lagged.

Agency and Direct auto policy counts reached near-equal balance, described as the 'policy equinox'; Robinson bundled customer new applications grew 50%+ quarter-over-quarter, aided by new Platinum product launch in Texas.

Platinum home-and-auto combined offering launched in Texas with planned expansion to additional states; new product design with advanced Snapshot discount estimation at point of sale rolled out across states.

Aggregate outlook for needed rate change was assessed as very low; management noted rising loss frequency trends but described current pricing as well-matched given prior rate actions, with no dramatic adjustments expected.

Loss frequency and other loss cost trends were described as definitely rising; current favorable results reflected pricing calibrated earlier; ongoing matching of price to future loss costs was identified as the primary challenge.

Year-to-date investment total return stood at just 1% on a $20+ billion portfolio amid market volatility from Greece and Puerto Rico concerns; short-duration strategy continued to limit downside from rate movements.

Platinum was designed as a 'must have' bundled offering for targeted agents serving Robinson customer segments; early results in Texas were described as satisfactorily generating incremental growth of the desired business type.
